lines.en_lit: that the sequestering of this for sanctity, His brethren, the priests, touch him, the sequestering of that for purity, and his brethren, the priests, not touch him. In accordance with whose either Rabbi Meir or Rabbi Yosei. As, if Rabbi Ḥanina, the deputy Priest, isn’t there also this

Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, strongly objects to Granted, first perhaps the third second perhaps the third third perhaps the third fifth perhaps the seventh sixth perhaps the seventh seventh perhaps the seventh
However, fourth why do I sprinkling at all? Neither with regard to third is there uncertainty, nor with regard to seventh is there uncertainty,

And to your reasoning, is there sprinkling all seven Don’t we maintain that sprinkling is rabbinic decree and does not override Shabbat. Rather, what have you to say? seven except for Shabbat. Here too, seven except for fourth

Rava said: Therefore, the High Priest on Yom Kippur, the matter is not dependent on us; rather, the matter is dependent on the determination of the month, required to remove on the third of Tishrei, and whenever the third of Tishrei occurs we remove him However, priest who burns the heifer, the matter is dependent on us, we remove him on the fourth day of the week, so that the fourth day of his will occur on Shabbat.
to the Chamber of Parhedrin. It was taught Rabbi Yehuda And was it the Chamber of Parhedrin, Wasn’t it the Chamber of Balvatei,

Rather, initially, they would call it the Chamber of Balvatei, However, because giving money to the Priesthood, replaced every twelve months like the parhedrin who are replaced every twelve months. Therefore, the chamber was called Parhedrin.
We learned there the Sages required bakers to separate only teruma of the tithe, ḥalla,
Granted, teruma gedola, not as it was taught
